input.initialPrompt: Analyze this project for incremental build issues. Identify any custom targets that would unnecessarily re-run on every build even when nothing has changed.

data.reasoningText: The user wants me to analyze the project for incremental build issues, specifically looking for custom targets that would unnecessarily re-run on every build even when nothing has changed. Let me look at the project files.

toolResult.textResultForLlm:
1. <Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
2.   <PropertyGroup>
3.     <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
4.   </PropertyGroup>
5. 
6.   <Target Name="GenerateTimestamp" BeforeTargets="CoreCompile">
7.     <PropertyGroup>
8.       <TimestampFile>$(IntermediateOutputPath)build-timestamp.txt</TimestampFile>
9.     </PropertyGroup>
10.     <WriteLinesToFile File="$(TimestampFile)" Lines="Built at: $([System.DateTime]::Now.ToString())" Overwrite="true" />
11.   </Target>
12. 
13.   <Target Name="EmbedGitHash" BeforeTargets="CoreCompile">
14.     <Exec Command="git rev-parse HEAD" ConsoleToMsBuild="true" IgnoreExitCode="true">
15.       <Output TaskParameter="ConsoleOutput" PropertyName="GitHash" />
16.     </Exec>
17.     <WriteLinesToFile File="$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs"
18.                      Lines="namespace IncrementalBroken { internal static class BuildInfo { public const string GitHash = "$(GitHash)"%3B } }"
19.                      Overwrite="true" />
20.     <ItemGroup>
21.       <Compile Include="$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs" />
22.     </ItemGroup>
23.   </Target>
24. </Project>
25.

2.   <PropertyGroup>

4.   </PropertyGroup>
5.

@@ -1,25 +1,25 @@
 <Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
   <PropertyGroup>
     <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
   </PropertyGroup>
 
   <Target Name="GenerateTimestamp" BeforeTargets="CoreCompile">
     <PropertyGroup>
       <TimestampFile>$(IntermediateOutputPath)build-timestamp.txt</TimestampFile>
     </PropertyGroup>
     <WriteLinesToFile File="$(TimestampFile)" Lines="Built at: $([System.DateTime]::Now.ToString())" Overwrite="true" />
   </Target>
 
   <Target Name="EmbedGitHash" BeforeTargets="CoreCompile">
     <Exec Command="git rev-parse HEAD" ConsoleToMsBuild="true" IgnoreExitCode="true">
       <Output TaskParameter="ConsoleOutput" PropertyName="GitHash" />
     </Exec>
     <WriteLinesToFile File="$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs"
                      Lines="namespace IncrementalBroken { internal static class BuildInfo { public const string GitHash = "$(GitHash)"%3B } }"
                      Overwrite="true" />
     <ItemGroup>
       <Compile Include="$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs" />
     </ItemGroup>
   </Target>
 </Project>

data.content:
## Incremental Build Issues Found

There are **two custom targets** that break incremental builds:

### 1. `GenerateTimestamp` (line 6–11)

- **Problem:** Writes `$([System.DateTime]::Now.ToString())` to `build-timestamp.txt` on every build. Because the content always changes (the time is always different), MSBuild sees the file as modified, which triggers `CoreCompile` to re-run every time — even if no source code changed.
- **Fix:** Either remove it, make it conditional on a property (e.g., only in CI), or use `Inputs`/`Outputs` attributes to skip when outputs are already up-to-date.

### 2. `EmbedGitHash` (line 13–23)

- **Problem:** Runs `git rev-parse HEAD` and writes the result to `git-hash.g.cs` **unconditionally**. Even if the commit hash hasn't changed, `WriteLinesToFile` with `Overwrite="true"` rewrites the file, updating its timestamp. This also invalidates `CoreCompile`.
- **Fix:** Use a technique that only writes when the content actually changes (e.g., write to a `.tmp` file, compare with the existing file, and only copy if different). Alternatively, add `Inputs`/`Outputs` with a sentinel, or gate on a `Condition`.

### Summary

Both targets lack `Inputs`/`Outputs` attributes and unconditionally produce new file content, causing the compiler to run on **every** build regardless of source changes.
